raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("visual cortex location")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the provided evidence, I strongly agree with my earlier conclusion (\boxed{A}). The visual cortex is definitively situated in the occipital lobe, as evidenced by numerous sources stating explicitly that the primary visual cortex ("Brodmann area 17" or V1) resides around the calcarine sulcus here. Furthermore, research indicates sequential processing starting from the thalamus and continuing through successive stages including V1, V2, etc., all within the confines of the occipital lobe. There isn't any conflicting information suggesting otherwise among these reliable references. Therefore, option B-C-D remain incorrect due to lack of presence outside just the occipital lobe.
